raw_consumer_statement: Summary of credit card FRAUD : I was traveling in XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX  and after I returned home, I discovered a fraudulent charge on my XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XX/XX/XXXX for {$5300.00} ( XXXX EUR ). The merchant is : XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. It appears this was done by a taxi we took. My card was never lost and I handed over the card in the taxi, but sadly I did not save any receipts and do not have record of the actual charge. When I discovered the fraud I immediately called Barclays Bank who cancelled the card, took the amount off of my account and started their investigation. After a few weeks I received a letter from Barclays that told me : " ... they completed their investigation and have determined the activity is valid because the card was identified as being in your possession and chip card technology was used... the provisional credit has been reversed and the amount owed has been placed back on your account ... '' WHAT!?!? I immediately called and pleaded my case. I did not spend this. This merchant is a strange transportation company of some sort, which includes taxis. I have NEVER spent this much. Please look at my history and this merchant. Barclays preceded to reopen the case, and then after another week or so, send me the same letter. This happened a number of times. I would receive the letter, call, case reopened, & then same letter. I also filed a police report, wrote letters, put a fraud alert on all my accounts ( through XXXX  ), filed a claim with my homeowners, contacted the US embassy in XXXX, contacted the Office of the President of Barclays, and am filing complaints with many of the regulatory agencies. I am doing everything I can do. I did pay my whole bill with the fraudulent charge on it ( was due XX/XX/XXXX ), so not to accrue interest and fees ( was counseled to do so ). I was told by Barclays on XX/XX/XXXX that due to the fact that I have no evidence and 2 months is up, the case is now closed. I am obviously upset and disappointed. I am now waiting to see if my homeowners, or any regulatory agencies, will help at all.
